summary: Single user is stuck in an MFA loop after valid credentials and has not yet tried token reset.
rationale: This matches a known single-user access issue with a documented first-line recovery path.

summary: User says password reset never arrives, and auth logs show messages sent to an outdated alias.
rationale: The issue matches a documented KB path and can be handled safely at first line.

summary: User reports VPN is broken but does not provide operating system, VPN client version, hostname, or any command output.
rationale: The report is too vague to choose a safe support path.

summary: Three users on one team started failing MFA sign-in within the last hour after a policy update.
rationale: This may be a multi-user policy issue and should not stay at first line.

summary: Expired-password users are being routed to the home page instead of the password-change flow.
rationale: This is a known defect affecting self-service recovery.

summary: A single user connects to VPN successfully but internal hostnames still fail after DNS flush and VPN profile reinstall.
rationale: The standard first-line path has already failed, so ownership should move up.
